army-effect.name: ArmyEffect
army-effect.definition: type ArmyEffect<A, E> = Effect.Effect<A, E | ArmyError | BridgeError>

keys.name: resolveItems
keys.description: When true, items will be resolved under the `items` key of the army config.

army-loop-taunt-options.name: ArmyLoopTauntOptions
army-loop-taunt-options.definition: type ArmyLoopTauntOptions = ArmyLoopTauntBaseOptions & { aura: string; debounceMs?: never; delayMs?: number; message?: never } | ArmyLoopTauntBaseOptions & { aura?: never; debounceMs?: number; delayMs?: never; message: string }

keys.name: stack
keys.description: Number of active instances tracked for this aura name.

keys.name: value
keys.description:
The aura's value, if applicable.
Can be an integer or float.

auth-connect-outcome.name: AuthConnectOutcome
auth-connect-outcome.definition: type AuthConnectOutcome = { message: string; retryable: false; serverName?: string; status: "connected" } | { message: string; retryable: boolean; serverName?: string; status: AuthConnectFailureStatus }

avatar.name: Avatar
avatar.summary: Represents a player in the world.

keys.name: alive
keys.description: Whether the entity is alive.

keys.name: cell
keys.description: The cell the entity is in.

keys.name: hp
keys.description: The entity's current HP.

keys.name: hpPercentage
keys.description: The entity's current HP percentage.

keys.name: level
keys.description: The player's level.

keys.name: maxHp
keys.description: The entity's max HP.

keys.name: maxMp
keys.description: The player's max mp.

keys.name: mp
keys.description: The player's current mp.

keys.name: mpPercentage
keys.description: The player's current MP percentage.

keys.name: pad
keys.description: The pad the player is in.

keys.name: position
keys.description: The player's position.

keys.name: state
keys.description: The entity's state.

keys.name: username
keys.description: The player's username.

keys.name: isAFK()
keys.description: Whether the player is AFK.

keys.name: isHpGreaterThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is greater than a value.

keys.name: isHpLessThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is less than a value.

keys.name: isHpPercentageGreaterThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is greater than a percentage value.

keys.name: isHpPercentageLessThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is less than a percentage value.

keys.name: isIdle()
keys.description: Whether the entity is idle.

keys.name: isInCell()
keys.description: Whether the entity is in the specified cell.

keys.name: isInCombat()
keys.description: Whether the entity is in combat.

keys.name: isMonster()
keys.description: Whether the entity is a monster.

keys.name: isPlayer()
keys.description: Whether the entity is a player.

avatar-data.name: AvatarData
avatar-data.definition: type AvatarData = BaseEntityData & { afk: boolean; entID: number; entType: string; intLevel: number; intMP: number; intMPMax: number; strPad: string; strUsername: string; tx: number; ty: number; uoName: string }

base-entity.name: BaseEntity
base-entity.summary: Base class for entities in the game world.

keys.name: alive
keys.description: Whether the entity is alive.

keys.name: cell
keys.description: The cell the entity is in.

keys.name: hp
keys.description: The entity's current HP.

keys.name: hpPercentage
keys.description: The entity's current HP percentage.

keys.name: maxHp
keys.description: The entity's max HP.

keys.name: state
keys.description: The entity's state.

keys.name: isHpGreaterThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is greater than a value.

keys.name: isHpLessThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is less than a value.

keys.name: isHpPercentageGreaterThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is greater than a percentage value.

keys.name: isHpPercentageLessThan()
keys.description: Whether the entity's hp is less than a percentage value.

keys.name: isIdle()
keys.description: Whether the entity is idle.

keys.name: isInCell()
keys.description: Whether the entity is in the specified cell.

keys.name: isInCombat()
keys.description: Whether the entity is in combat.

keys.name: isMonster()
keys.description: Whether the entity is a monster.

collection.name: Collection
collection.summary: A Map with additional utility methods.
collection.definition: class Collection<Key, Value> extends Map<Key, Value>

keys.name: constructor
keys.description: Ambient declaration to allow references to `this.constructor` in class methods.

keys.name: [iterator]()
keys.description: Returns an iterable of entries in the map.

keys.name: at()
keys.description:
Identical to Array.at().
Returns the item at a given index, allowing for positive and negative integers.
Negative integers count back from the last item in the collection.

keys.name: clone()
keys.description: Creates an identical shallow copy of this collection.

keys.name: concat()
keys.description: Combines this collection with others into a new collection. None of the source collections are modified.

keys.name: difference()
keys.description: Returns a new collection containing the items where the key is present in this collection but not the other.

keys.name: each()
keys.signature: each(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => void): this
keys.description:
Identical to
Map.forEach(),
but returns the collection instead of undefined.

keys.name: each()
keys.signature: each<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => void, thisArg: This): this
keys.description:
Identical to
Map.forEach(),
but returns the collection instead of undefined.

keys.name: ensure()
keys.signature: ensure(key: Key, defaultValueGenerator: (key: Key, collection: this) => Value): Value
keys.description: Obtains the value of the given key if it exists, otherwise sets and returns the value provided by the default value generator.

keys.name: entries()
keys.description: Returns an iterable of key, value pairs for every entry in the map.

keys.name: equals()
keys.description:
Checks if this collection shares identical items with another.
This is different to checking for equality using equal-signs, because
the collections may be different objects, but contain the same data.

keys.name: every()
keys.signature: every<NewKey>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey): this is Collection<NewKey, Value>
keys.description:
Checks if all items passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.every().

keys.name: every()
keys.signature: every<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue): this is Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Checks if all items passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.every().

keys.name: every()
keys.signature: every(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): boolean
keys.description:
Checks if all items passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.every().

keys.name: every()
keys.signature: every<This, NewKey>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey, thisArg: This): this is Collection<NewKey, Value>
keys.description:
Checks if all items passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.every().

keys.name: every()
keys.signature: every<This, NewValue>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue, thisArg: This): this is Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Checks if all items passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.every().

keys.name: every()
keys.signature: every<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): boolean
keys.description:
Checks if all items passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.every().

keys.name: filter()
keys.signature: filter<NewKey>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey): Collection<NewKey, Value>
keys.description:
Identical to
Array.filter(),
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: filter()
keys.signature: filter<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue): Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Identical to
Array.filter(),
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: filter()
keys.signature: filter(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): Collection<Key, Value>
keys.description:
Identical to
Array.filter(),
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: filter()
keys.signature: filter<This, NewKey>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey, thisArg: This): Collection<NewKey, Value>
keys.description:
Identical to
Array.filter(),
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: filter()
keys.signature: filter<This, NewValue>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue, thisArg: This): Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Identical to
Array.filter(),
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: filter()
keys.signature: filter<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): Collection<Key, Value>
keys.description:
Identical to
Array.filter(),
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: find()
keys.signature: find<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue): NewValue | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.find().

keys.name: find()
keys.signature: find(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): Value | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.find().

keys.name: find()
keys.signature: find<This, NewValue>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue, thisArg: This): NewValue | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.find().

keys.name: find()
keys.signature: find<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): Value | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.find().

keys.name: findKey()
keys.signature: findKey<NewKey>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey): NewKey | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: findKey()
keys.signature: findKey(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): Key | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: findKey()
keys.signature: findKey<This, NewKey>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey, thisArg: This): NewKey | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: findKey()
keys.signature: findKey<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): Key | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a single item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: findLast()
keys.signature: findLast<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue): NewValue | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLast().

keys.name: findLast()
keys.signature: findLast(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): Value | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLast().

keys.name: findLast()
keys.signature: findLast<This, NewValue>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue, thisArg: This): NewValue | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLast().

keys.name: findLast()
keys.signature: findLast<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): Value | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLast().

keys.name: findLastKey()
keys.signature: findLastKey<NewKey>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey): NewKey | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLastIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: findLastKey()
keys.signature: findLastKey(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): Key | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLastIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: findLastKey()
keys.signature: findLastKey<This, NewKey>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey, thisArg: This): NewKey | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLastIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: findLastKey()
keys.signature: findLastKey<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): Key | undefined
keys.description:
Searches for the key of a last item where the given function returns a truthy value. This behaves like
Array.findLastIndex(),
but returns the key rather than the positional index.

keys.name: first()
keys.description: Obtains the first value(s) in this collection.

keys.name: first()
keys.description: Obtains the first value(s) in this collection.

keys.name: firstKey()
keys.description: Obtains the first key(s) in this collection.

keys.name: firstKey()
keys.description: Obtains the first key(s) in this collection.

keys.name: flatMap()
keys.signature: flatMap<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => Collection<Key, NewValue>): Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Maps each item into a Collection, then joins the results into a single Collection. Identical in behavior to
Array.flatMap().

keys.name: flatMap()
keys.signature: flatMap<NewValue, This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => Collection<Key, NewValue>, thisArg: This): Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Maps each item into a Collection, then joins the results into a single Collection. Identical in behavior to
Array.flatMap().

keys.name: forEach()
keys.signature: forEach(callbackfn: (value: Value, key: Key, map: Map<Key, Value>) => void, thisArg?: any): void
keys.description: Executes a provided function once per each key/value pair in the Map, in insertion order.

keys.name: get()
keys.description: Returns a specified element from the Map object. If the value that is associated to the provided key is an object, then you will get a reference to that object and any change made to that object will effectively modify it inside the Map.

keys.name: hasAll()
keys.description: Checks if all of the elements exist in the collection.

keys.name: hasAny()
keys.description: Checks if any of the elements exist in the collection.

keys.name: intersection()
keys.description: The intersection method returns a new collection containing the items where the key is present in both collections.

keys.name: keyAt()
keys.description:
Identical to Array.at().
Returns the key at a given index, allowing for positive and negative integers.
Negative integers count back from the last item in the collection.

keys.name: keys()
keys.description: Returns an iterable of keys in the map

keys.name: last()
keys.description: Obtains the last value(s) in this collection.

keys.name: last()
keys.description: Obtains the last value(s) in this collection.

keys.name: lastKey()
keys.description: Obtains the last key(s) in this collection.

keys.name: lastKey()
keys.description: Obtains the last key(s) in this collection.

keys.name: map()
keys.signature: map<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => NewValue): NewValue[]
keys.description:
Maps each item to another value into an array. Identical in behavior to
Array.map().

keys.name: map()
keys.signature: map<This, NewValue>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => NewValue, thisArg: This): NewValue[]
keys.description:
Maps each item to another value into an array. Identical in behavior to
Array.map().

keys.name: mapValues()
keys.signature: mapValues<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => NewValue): Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Maps each item to another value into a collection. Identical in behavior to
Array.map().

keys.name: mapValues()
keys.signature: mapValues<This, NewValue>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => NewValue, thisArg: This): Collection<Key, NewValue>
keys.description:
Maps each item to another value into a collection. Identical in behavior to
Array.map().

keys.name: merge()
keys.signature: merge<OtherValue, ResultValue>(other: ReadonlyCollection<Key, OtherValue>, whenInSelf: (value: Value, key: Key) => Keep<ResultValue>, whenInOther: (valueOther: OtherValue, key: Key) => Keep<ResultValue>, whenInBoth: (value: Value, valueOther: OtherValue, key: Key) => Keep<ResultValue>): Collection<Key, ResultValue>
keys.description: Merges two Collections together into a new Collection.

keys.name: partition()
keys.signature: partition<NewKey>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey): [Collection<NewKey, Value>, Collection<Exclude<Key, NewKey>, Value>]
keys.description:
Partitions the collection into two collections where the first collection
contains the items that passed and the second contains the items that failed.

keys.name: partition()
keys.signature: partition<NewValue>(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue): [Collection<Key, NewValue>, Collection<Key, Exclude<Value, NewValue>>]
keys.description:
Partitions the collection into two collections where the first collection
contains the items that passed and the second contains the items that failed.

keys.name: partition()
keys.signature: partition(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): [Collection<Key, Value>, Collection<Key, Value>]
keys.description:
Partitions the collection into two collections where the first collection
contains the items that passed and the second contains the items that failed.

keys.name: partition()
keys.signature: partition<This, NewKey>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => key is NewKey, thisArg: This): [Collection<NewKey, Value>, Collection<Exclude<Key, NewKey>, Value>]
keys.description:
Partitions the collection into two collections where the first collection
contains the items that passed and the second contains the items that failed.

keys.name: partition()
keys.signature: partition<This, NewValue>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => value is NewValue, thisArg: This): [Collection<Key, NewValue>, Collection<Key, Exclude<Value, NewValue>>]
keys.description:
Partitions the collection into two collections where the first collection
contains the items that passed and the second contains the items that failed.

keys.name: partition()
keys.signature: partition<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): [Collection<Key, Value>, Collection<Key, Value>]
keys.description:
Partitions the collection into two collections where the first collection
contains the items that passed and the second contains the items that failed.

keys.name: random()
keys.description: Obtains unique random value(s) from this collection.

keys.name: random()
keys.description: Obtains unique random value(s) from this collection.

keys.name: randomKey()
keys.description: Obtains unique random key(s) from this collection.

keys.name: randomKey()
keys.description: Obtains unique random key(s) from this collection.

keys.name: reduce()
keys.signature: reduce(fn: (accumulator: Value, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => Value, initialValue?: Value): Value
keys.description:
Applies a function to produce a single value. Identical in behavior to
Array.reduce().

keys.name: reduce()
keys.signature: reduce<InitialValue>(fn: (accumulator: InitialValue, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => InitialValue, initialValue: InitialValue): InitialValue
keys.description:
Applies a function to produce a single value. Identical in behavior to
Array.reduce().

keys.name: reduceRight()
keys.signature: reduceRight(fn: (accumulator: Value, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => Value, initialValue?: Value): Value
keys.description:
Applies a function to produce a single value. Identical in behavior to
Array.reduceRight().

keys.name: reduceRight()
keys.signature: reduceRight<InitialValue>(fn: (accumulator: InitialValue, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => InitialValue, initialValue: InitialValue): InitialValue
keys.description:
Applies a function to produce a single value. Identical in behavior to
Array.reduceRight().

keys.name: reverse()
keys.description:
Identical to Array.reverse()
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: set()
keys.description: Adds a new element with a specified key and value to the Map. If an element with the same key already exists, the element will be updated.

keys.name: some()
keys.signature: some(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): boolean
keys.description:
Checks if there exists an item that passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.some().

keys.name: some()
keys.signature: some<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): boolean
keys.description:
Checks if there exists an item that passes a test. Identical in behavior to
Array.some().

keys.name: sort()
keys.description:
The sort method sorts the items of a collection in place and returns it.
If a comparison function is not provided, the function sorts by element values, using the same stringwise comparison algorithm as
Array.sort().

keys.name: sweep()
keys.signature: sweep(fn: (value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown): number
keys.description: Removes items that satisfy the provided filter function.

keys.name: sweep()
keys.signature: sweep<This>(fn: (this: This, value: Value, key: Key, collection: this) => unknown, thisArg: This): number
keys.description: Removes items that satisfy the provided filter function.

keys.name: symmetricDifference()
keys.signature: symmetricDifference<OtherValue>(other: ReadonlyCollection<Key, OtherValue>): Collection<Key, Value | OtherValue>
keys.description: Returns a new collection containing only the items where the keys are present in either collection, but not both.

keys.name: tap()
keys.description: Runs a function on the collection and returns the collection.

keys.name: tap()
keys.signature: tap<This>(fn: (this: This, collection: this) => void, thisArg: This): this
keys.description: Runs a function on the collection and returns the collection.

keys.name: toReversed()
keys.description:
Identical to Array.toReversed()
but returns a Collection instead of an Array.

keys.name: toSorted()
keys.description:
The toSorted method returns a shallow copy of the collection with the items sorted.
If a comparison function is not provided, the function sorts by element values, using the same stringwise comparison algorithm as
Array.sort().

keys.name: union()
keys.signature: union<OtherValue>(other: ReadonlyCollection<Key, OtherValue>): Collection<Key, Value | OtherValue>
keys.description: Returns a new collection containing the items where the key is present in either of the collections.

keys.name: values()
keys.description: Returns an iterable of values in the map

comparator.name: Comparator
comparator.definition: type Comparator<Key, Value> = (firstValue: Value, secondValue: Value, firstKey: Key, secondKey: Key) => number

keys.name: Dead
keys.description: The entity is dead.

keys.name: Idle
keys.description: The entity is idle.

keys.name: InCombat
keys.description: The entity is in combat.

environment-drop-policy.name: EnvironmentDropPolicy
environment-drop-policy.summary: Handling options for drops that are not registered by name.

keys.name: acceptAcMemberOnlyDrops
keys.description: Accept member-only AC-tagged items.

keys.name: acceptAcNonMemberDrops
keys.description: Accept non-member AC-tagged items.

keys.name: acceptNonAcMemberOnlyDrops
keys.description: Accept member-only non-AC items.

keys.name: acceptNonAcNonMemberDrops
keys.description: Accept non-member non-AC items.

keys.name: rejectUnregisteredDrops
keys.description: Reject any unregistered drop that is not accepted by this policy.

keys.name: enhancement
keys.description: The base enhancement name to match, such as "lucky" or "forge".

keys.name: slot
keys.description: The slot of the item, one of "weapon", "cape", "helm", "class".

keys.name: special
keys.description: Optional special enhancement variant, such as a weapon proc or Forge special ("anima", "vainglory").

keys.name: data
keys.description: Data about this faction.

keys.name: id
keys.description: The ID of the faction.

keys.name: name
keys.description: The in-game name of the faction.

keys.name: rank
keys.description: The rank that the player has achieved in this faction.

keys.name: remainingRep
keys.description: The remaining amount of rep required for the player to rank up.

keys.name: rep
keys.description: The amount of rep the player has for their current rank.

keys.name: requiredRep
keys.description: The total required rep for the player to rank up.

keys.name: totalRep
keys.description: The total amount of rep the player has for this faction.

keys.name: FactionID
keys.description: The ID of the faction.

keys.name: iRank
keys.description: The rank that the player has achieved in this faction.

keys.name: iRep
keys.description: The total amount of rep the player has for this faction.

keys.name: iRepToRank
keys.description: The total required rep for the player to rank up.

keys.name: iSpillRep
keys.description: The amount of rep the player has for their current rank.

keys.name: sName
keys.description: The name of the faction.

inventory-item-selector.name: InventoryItemSelector
inventory-item-selector.definition: type InventoryItemSelector = { itemId?: number; name: string } | { itemId: number; name?: string }

item.name: Item
item.summary: The base class for all-things item related.

keys.name: category
keys.description: The category of the item.

keys.name: charItemId
keys.description: The character ID of this item.

keys.name: classRank
keys.description: The class rank represented by this item's accumulated class points.

keys.name: data
keys.description: Data about this item

keys.name: description
keys.description: The description of the item.

keys.name: enhancementLevel
keys.description: The enhancement level of the item.

keys.name: enhancementPatternId
keys.description: The enhancement pattern ID of the item.

keys.name: fileLink
keys.description: The link to the source file of the item

keys.name: fileName
keys.description: The name of the source file of the item.

keys.name: id
keys.description: The ID of the item.

keys.name: itemGroup
keys.description: The group of the item.

readonly-collection.summary: Represents an immutable version of a collection

script-event-listener.name: ScriptEventListener
script-event-listener.definition: type ScriptEventListener<E> = (event: ScriptEventMap[E]) => void | Effect.Effect<unknown, unknown> | Generator<Effect.Yieldable<any, any, never, never>, unknown, never>

script-event-predicate.name: ScriptEventPredicate
script-event-predicate.definition: type ScriptEventPredicate<E> = (event: ScriptEventMap[E]) => boolean | Effect.Effect<boolean, unknown> | Generator<Effect.Yieldable<any, any, never, never>, boolean, never>
